title: Estimate Scope of Work
content_md:
# Estimate Scope of Work

- Code: 237288c0
- Source: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=237288c02fcd4d77960d16fc7bc637b1
- Video: https://www.loom.com/share/96581d85ac584f7597b3c069f7977ab7?hide_owner=true&hide_share=true&hide_title=true&hideEmbedTopBar=true
- Duration: 2m 54s

## Description

_(no description)_

transcript.text: So I'm just going to walk through an example of how we estimate scope of work that we might need for a new prospect, new client.
So the first thing that we're going to do is run a local rank map, right?
We want to see where are they currently ranked? Do we need to build topical relevance? Do we need to build geographical relevance? Where are they?
Okay, and it's important to know that if the rank map looks pretty good, we'll just jump straight into geographic relevance.
Even if they don't have the core 30 done, which they almost never do, we'll still jump straight into geographical relevance because they need geographical help.
The rank map looks pretty good. We just have a couple of spots to work on.
Okay, so once we've done the local rank map, then we're just going to do a site colon search for them and for the top three competitors.
Because we're really just looking to estimate how big is this website.
So let me pull this up. I'm just doing a quick example here for plumber in Fort Wayne, Indiana.
Excellent. Okay, so here are the three that are currently ranking.
So we'll open up those three websites. I'm holding down control to open them all up.
And I will go here, copy the URL. I'll scroll down so you can see copy the URL and then site colon URL.
Now, I'm going to get rid of that landing page. This is either a multi-location or it is a franchise.
Their landing page is not the homepage, but let's do that to see how big the site is.
We have 230. And now I'll do it again for this one.
Get tools. We have 10. And then here we have another multi-location business or franchise and we have 94.
So based on those three results, we have 10. We have 230. We have 94.
My guess would be that to rank in the top three, we'd probably want to see 50, 60 pages, something in that range.
So what we would then do is think about how long do I want that to take?
Do I want it to be three months? Do I want it to be six months? Do I want it to be 12 months?
So if we wanted to be six months, we have 50 or 60 pages. We have six months.
So I'm going to estimate about 10 pages per month and that would be my scope of work for a plumber who wants to rank in Fort Wayne, Indiana.
If instead we said, man, we want to get them in three months, then I would say we need 20 pages in order to hit that 60 in three months.
So real quick, that is how we estimate a scope of work for a new client.
We don't want to spend hours and hours doing this. We want to just get a decent reasonable estimate.
So that way we can put the pricing, put the proposal together and send it off to the client.
If we spend hours and hours evaluating every single client, then we're never going to get anything else done.
So you've got to be reasonably quick about this.

summary: This lesson explains how to estimate the scope of work for a new client by analyzing their current ranking and competitor sites. The goal is to quickly determine the number of pages needed to rank in top positions within a given timeframe.
key_points: Run a local rank map first
Estimate page count based on results
Quickly estimate scope for efficient proposals
